item.title: Old Fortnite Battle Pass Skins Are Coming Back: Here's What We Know
item.description:
There are thousands of cosmetic items in Fortnite , but only a few of them are exclusive and will remain unobtainable. Battle Pass skins had always been promoted as exclusive, meaning that there would be no way to get them once the pass became available. However, back in Chapter 5 Season 3, Epic Games announced that this would be changing the following season.

According to the rules that Epic Games established, skins given away in passes would only be made available 18 months following that pass's end date. That time is quickly approaching, and this guide tells players what they need to know about the return of Battle Pass skins in Fortnite .

When Can Battle Pass Skins Return to Fortnite via the Item Shop?

Epic Games announced the changes being made to item exclusivity in August 2024, two weeks before the end of Chapter 5 Season 3. The reason behind this change was the fact that certain skins, mainly known characters from collabs, were locked and could not be obtained by players, as they were Battle Pass rewards.

The official announcement post mentioned that these changes would only be applicable to the cosmetics featured in passes following the announcement. This means that Battle Pass items that were released after August 7, 2024, can return to Fortnite 18 months after they were last available through a pass .

Which Battle Pass Skins May Return to Fortnite?

The passes that went live following the announcement ended on November 2, 2024, which means that they may return to Fortnite as Item Shop items after May 2, 2026. Here are the cosmetics that may appear in the Item Shop, as they were released after August 2024:

  • Battle Pass cosmetics from Chapter 5 Season 4 onwards
  • Music Pass cosmetics from Season 5 onwards
  • LEGO Pass cosmetics from Tropical Treasures onwards

One thing that Epic Games notes about this return is that bonus styles and super styles may be sold alongside these skins, but that is not a guarantee. Additionally, it is likely that some skins won't ever be made available again in the shop, even though they can. Cosmetics that resulted from collabs have a much higher chance to return, as they were the reason why this change happened in the first place.

Fortnite

If players are curious about the return of a specific item, all they need to do to figure out when it may return is check out when its pass ended and then add 18 months to that date.

item.title: Fortnite Confirms Downtime for First Big Update of Chapter 7 Season 2
item.description:
Fortnite has revealed when downtime will begin for this season's first major update, patch v40.10. On March 19, Fortnite players said goodbye to Chapter 7 Season 1 and welcomed Chapter 7 Season 2. This season is titled Fortnite Showdown, as it is focused on the fight between The Foundation and Ice King. Each player had to pick which side they would be on for the rest of the season when they first logged into Fortnite in Chapter 7 Season 2. During Battle Royale matches, players are assigned rivals from the opposite team and are tasked with eliminating them in order to earn points for their team.

The start of a season is always exciting, as players get to explore new mechanics and weapons, meet all the non-playable characters scattered around the map, and uncover a brand-new storyline. However, players aren't immediately introduced to all the seasonal content at the start, to keep them interested throughout the three months that it is live. Chapter 7 Season 2 has been live for nearly two weeks, and players are ready for more content. Players have completed many in-game rivalries and are nearly done with all Act 1 milestones and have become acquainted with the new Chapter 7 Season 2 mechanics. Therefore, it's time for Epic Games to drop the next content patch.

Fortnite Reveals When Downtime for v40.10 Begins

  • Downtime start time for v40.10: April 1 at 4 AM ET

In January, Fortnite 's 2026 update schedule was shared on its Trello page, so players knew that an update was coming this Wednesday. However, they didn't know when downtime would begin, as Epic Games only tends to share that information a few hours beforehand. The official Fortnite Status account has shared on social media that downtime for patch v40.10 will begin at 4 AM ET on April 1. This means that players have until about 3:30 AM ET to play Fortnite tonight, as matchmaking is usually disabled 30 minutes prior to servers shutting down to prevent players from being kicked out of a game.

What's Coming to Fortnite This Update?

  • Chapter 1 Season 8 OG Pass
  • Endgame LTM
  • Brainrot skins (rumored)

Even though this is only the first update since Chapter 7 Season 2 began, players already have a lot to look forward to. Plenty of leaks and official news have come out regarding what's coming in this update, which has made fans quite eager. The biggest part of the update is the release of Chapter 1 Season 8 of Fortnite OG. Fortnite Crew members and players who enjoy this game mode will have a brand-new Pass to unlock, map changes to explore, and weapons and mechanics that they haven't played with since the season's original launch in 2019. This season also introduced players to the Endgame LTM, and it will once again be made available to players on April 3. Players have a couple of days to get acquainted with the map and weapon changes, unlock a few OG Pass rewards, and then focus on the returning game mode.

In addition, the long-awaited brainrot skins are expected to drop with the launch of this Fortnite update. Tung Tung Tung Sahur and Ballerina Cappucina were seen in the Chapter 7 Season 2 trailer, and rumors point towards their release date being April 1. This coincides with April Fool's Day, so the launch of silly, viral meme skins would make sense. However, the exact release date of the brainrot skins coming to Fortnite is not confirmed yet, but players who are curious to know more should keep an eye on the leaks. As soon as servers go down, dataminers will be hard at work examining the game files so they can provide fans with information on what's coming this update, and this will likely include the Item Shop date for the brainrot skins.

Fortnite

Play thousands of free creator made islands with friends including deathruns, tycoons, racing, zombie survival and more! Join the creator community and build your own island with Unreal Editor for Fortnite (UEFN) or Fortnite Creative tools.

Each Fortnite island has an individual age rating so you can find the one that's right for you and your friends. Find it all in Fortnite!

item.title: It's Official: Rec Room is Shutting Down
item.description:
The developers of Rec Room have announced that the game will be shutting down this year. While Rec Room had a good amount of popularity, its run will come to an end a decade after it got its start.

Rec Room has frequently been compared to titles like Roblox , as the game offered social experiences in a sandbox that allowed players to create their own rooms. VR games remain a niche subgenre, but between VR headset owners and those who accessed the game in a 2D format, like through Rec Room 's Switch release, Rec Room gained 150 million players overall. However, these impressive numbers weren't enough to save the game.

Rec Room Announces Permanent Shutdown

On March 30, 2026, Rec Room announced that the game would be winding down and completely closing on June 1. The team explained that while the game had indeed reached over 150 million players, with millions still playing per month, the studio was never able to figure out how to turn a profit on the game. As a result, they have been spending more on maintaining the title and running it than they've earned. Additionally, the team cited changes in the VR market, as well as the shifts in gaming overall, as part of the reason for their decision to shut down.

Gamers who have active Rec Room accounts will be able to continue playing until the day of shutdown, but those who have never played it before won't get a chance to. Effective immediately, no new accounts can be created for the game, friends can't be added, and the Rec Room+ subscription service has been closed to new subscribers. Those who currently have the membership will see it extended to the game's shutdown. Partnered Creators can still cash out their tokens, and those who are owed Room Rewards from March will receive them in April. As a way of showing the team's appreciation to the players, all first-party content available in the stores has been discounted by 80%, and some of the Rec Room+ subscriber features have been made available to everyone.

While fans and creators are likely sad to see the game shut down, Rec Room is also taking steps to give players a way of remembering their time in the game, and to preserve rooms created. Players can download any photos they took and get a "final report card" that serves as a memento of their time in the title. Users on the Steam PC version of the game will be able to download their rooms starting in one week from the announcement. The data can be used to rebuild the rooms elsewhere, like in the Unity game engine. However, users should make sure to do so before the game shuts down, as the data will become inaccessible after that.

Rec Room was enjoyed across a number of platforms, but never came to PS VR2, despite being available for the original PS VR. The developers of the game explained back in 2024 that they couldn't justify the cost of creating the "non-trivial port" to PS VR2, even though they would have liked to. It might have been for the best, as the PS VR2 struggled to sell, with Sony cutting back production of the headset shortly after. Even if that hadn't been the case, though, it seems that there was an inherent issue with making money from Rec Room that may have meant the game would have shut down even sooner had the developer poured the resources into making another VR port.

At one time, the future of Rec Room looked bright, with a number of investors pouring money into the project. Rec Room raised at least $294 million between 2016 and 2021, ultimately boosting the developer studio to a valuation of $3.5 billion, but the investments ceased past that point. In 2025, the developer began cutting jobs, with layoff waves in March and August, the latter of which cut half of the studio's staff. Fans of Rec Room should consider popping in to say goodbye to the game before it becomes the latest game to shut down in 2026.

Rec Room

Follow
Followed
Arcade
Systems
Released
May 21, 2019

item.title: Crimson Desert: How to Solve Monolith Crown Abyss Cube Puzzle
item.description:
Monolith Crown is an Abyss Puzzle in Crimson Desert that players can access after returning to the Crow's Nest Abyss location after the Crowcaller boss fight. There's a large metallic cube in the area that has several light beams on its sides, and players need to rotate it in such a manner that each light beam hits a blue glowing stone on the walls. It's one of the more complicated abyss puzzles, as the position of light beams shifts when you rotate the cube, and this guide will show you the easiest way to solve the puzzle.

Monolith Crown Cube Abyss Puzzle Solution in Crimson Desert

The Monolith Crown Abyss Puzzle is part of the Spire of Soaring Abyss puzzles that players will eventually access during the Black and White questline in Crimson Desert's Chapter 5: Guest Unbidden.

Once players finish the Crowcaller boss fight, they'll return to land to finish off the chapter. However, players can later fast travel back to the Crow's Nest Abyss Nexus and access the next Abyss Puzzle in the Triangle Ring, called Monolith Crown .

Once you activate the Abyss Nexus fast travel point in Monolith Crown, head forward into the structure and keep going until you arrive at the Abyss gate. Turn left from the Abyss Gate, and you'll see a floating platform next to it that you can glide to and use your Axiom Force to activate a switch to start rotating the large cube puzzle .

Now you can control the large cube in the middle of the room. The main goal is to direct all the light beams coming off the sides of the cube toward the blue-glowing stones on the wall.

However, the light beams on the side of the cube can shift position depending on how the cube is rotated. While it's tricky, you can check out the solution below to quickly solve the Monolith Crown cube Abyss Puzzle in Crimson Desert .

Make sure the cube is back to its default position before following the solution below. You can reset the puzzle back to its original state by simply fast-traveling to the Monolith Crown's Abyss Nexus fast travel node.

  1. From the starting position, turn the Camera to "Right" and press the D-Pad "Right" twice .
  2. Turn the Camera "Left" and press the D-Pad "Up" button once .
  3. Turn the Camera "Left" again, and press the D-Pad "Up" button .
  4. Turn the Camera to "Right" , and press the D-Pad "Up" button .
  5. Rotate the Camera back to "Left" , and press the D-Pad "Up" button .
  6. Finally, press the D-Pad "Left" button without rotating the camera.

Once you're done, the main Abyss Mechanism on top will be activated. All you need to do is head back up to the Abyss Gate and use the Force Palm on the mechanism to complete the Monolith Crown laser cube puzzle in Crimson Desert .

Crimson Desert

item.title: Rumor: This Could Be the Persona 6 Reveal Date
item.description:
Fans hungry for the first reveal of Persona 6 may be in luck, as a rumor suggests that the reveal could take place on June 7. Although Atlus has kept a tight lid on Persona 6 and its ongoing development, an upcoming showcase could be the company's first opportunity to potentially give audiences a glimpse into the future of the Persona series.

Originally released as a spin-off to Atlus' popular Megami Tensei franchise, the first Persona title was released in 1996 on the PlayStation as Revelations: Persona . Since then, the Persona franchise has grown in popularity, with more mainline and spin-off entries releasing in Persona 's 30 years on the market. However, the previous mainline release of the Persona series was 2016's Persona 5 on the PS3 and PS4. An enhanced re-release, Persona 5 Royal , launched in 2019. Since then, Atlus released Persona 3 Reload in 2024, and Persona 4 Revival is currently in development with no release date information as of this writing.

Rumor: Persona 6 Could Appear During the Xbox Games Showcase on June 7

Though all rumors about Persona 6 should be taken with a fine grain of salt, one insider corroborated some information that was shared with them. According to gaming industry insider eXtas1s on Twitter, the reveal of Persona 6 could happen this year, with the upcoming Xbox Games Showcase on June 7 being a possible place for Sega to showcase the next entry of the Persona series. The insider pointed to Atlus and Microsoft having a multi-year partnership, and said that Atlus has used the Xbox Games Showcase in previous years as a platform for announcing some of its game projects. Persona 4 Revival was the most recent example, as it was officially revealed during the 2025 Xbox Games Showcase. However, eXtas1s later clarified that they were not the original source of the Persona 6 reveal date rumors and said that they simply relayed the information based on other reports from different sources.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

As for what fans can expect during the 2026 Xbox Games Showcase, Microsoft first-party titles like Fable and Halo: Campaign Evolved are likely to make appearances during the broadcast. Along with the main show, the Xbox Games Showcase will be immediately followed by a Gears of War: E-Day Direct broadcast hosted by The Coalition. The 2026 Xbox Games Showcase airs on Sunday, June 7, at 1:00 PM Eastern, on the official Xbox Twitch and YouTube channels. The Xbox show will air a few days after Summer Game Fest 2026, which will air on Friday, June 5, at 5:00 PM Eastern from the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles on Twitch and YouTube. Details about both the Xbox Games Showcase and Summer Game Fest will likely be revealed closer to the listed air dates.

Persona 6 Has Other Reveal Windows to Consider

Despite the lack of concrete Persona 6 news, Persona fans still have one confirmed release to look forward to in the future, as Persona 4 Revival remains in active development. Previous rumors stated that Persona 4 Revival could launch within Atlus' upcoming fiscal year, which begins April 1, 2026, and ends March 31, 2027. The same rumor stated that pre-orders for Persona 4 Revival could begin sometime in April 2026, though Atlus has not officially confirmed if that would be the case.

Although the 2026 Xbox Games Showcase is a possible window for Atlus to show off more of the Persona series, other gaming industry events like Summer Game Fest and Tokyo Game Show are ones that fans should circle on their calendars. Additionally, the Persona franchise's 30th anniversary date is September 30, 2026, though Atlus has so far not revealed if any events will take place on that day. Even though the rumors about Persona 6 show no signs of slowing down, time will tell when Atlus will show its hand.

Persona 4 Revival

Follow
Followed

Adventure
Systems

4 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Persona 4 Revival intertwines an investigation into a string of murders with the journey of self-discovery and encounters with the occult, all within a critically acclaimed, classic RPG experience—reborn in a new form!

In the rural Japanese town of Inaba, the once sleepy streets receive a cold wake-up call when a mysterious serial killer strikes. Also, what's with the rumor of a mysterious TV channel appearing only on rainy nights? A whirlwind of surprises and otherworldly adventures will make the next year of your high school life anything but ordinary!
